title: Multithreading & Concurrency
description: Understand execution threads, mutual exclusions (mutexes), lock guards, race conditions, and lock deadlocks.
learningObjectives: Synchronize shared variable access using std::mutex and std::lock_guard
Identify concurrency bugs like race conditions and thread lock deadlocks

sections.title: Thread Synchronization and Locking Primitives
sections.content:
When multiple threads read and write to the same memory location simultaneously, data corruption occurs (Race Condition). To prevent this, we use Mutual Exclusions (`std::mutex`). A thread locks the mutex, accesses the shared resource, and unlocks it. 

Locking introduces overhead, and if Thread A is waiting for a lock held by Thread B while Thread B is waiting for a lock held by Thread A, execution freezes entirely (Deadlock). Standardizing on RAII locks (`std::lock_guard`) prevents leaving locks open.

sections.title: Safe Thread Writes via Lock Guards
